chunks.content:
Article: EoE Ritsukos Last Scene Gendos Unheard Line
[]: EoE Ritsuko's Last Scene: Gendo's Unheard Line             Quote Post by MongolSquad » Sat Aug 21, 2004 3:35 pm As you all remember, in the finall moments of Ritsuko's lfe, Gendo says something but, it is unable to be heard. I've always wanted to discuss the many possibilites of what could have been said. I think one of the strongest theories is that it had something to do with the realtionship between Gendo and Ritsuko. But for some reason I feel that there was something more to that statement then Gendo saying something shocking involving love. I could be wrong, but this is up in the air still. Any other ideas?

[]:             Quote Post by Treeves056 » Sat Aug 21, 2004 3:42 pm A) Speculation abounds over what Gendo's last words to Ritsuko were - and there's simply no answer. In the original story boards, Gendo's line is supposed to be covered by the sound of an explosion (from the battle raging in NERV). While the explosion sound was eventually scrapped, Anno ensured that Gendo's line indeed drops out so that we cannot hear it. Yuriko Yamaguchi, Ritsuko's Japanese voice actress, too wondered what Gendo had told Ritsuko. But for her, it was a more personal question - because what Gendo told Risuko would directly dictate how the response, "Liar." was delivered. In the Red Cross Book (seiyuu section), Yamaguchi writes: Ritsuko fades away with her final word, "Liar." But what was this "Liar" in reference to? The script for this last scene only has Gendo saying: "Ritsuko Akagi, I truly...." followed by Ritsuko saying: "Liar (gets shot)". I can imagine many words that might follow "I truly....", but I can't decide on any in particular. That is the complexity of Gendou and Ritsuko's relationship. From Ritsuko Akagi's inner feelings as a scientist, she could be considered a woman who blindly gave her love to Gendou Ikari, and also a foolish woman that walked the same path as her mother Naoko who committed suicide after being betrayed by Ikari. I personally wanted her to end as a convenient, submissive woman

Article: EoE Ritsukos Last Scene Gendos Unheard Line
 woman who blindly gave her love to Gendou Ikari, and also a foolish woman that walked the same path as her mother Naoko who committed suicide after being betrayed by Ikari. I personally wanted her to end as a convenient, submissive woman who simply wanted to die righteously. But in the previous movie (D&R) she ended as a deeply jealous woman filled with nothing but hatred toward Ikari. Feeling unsatisfied with this, I looked for a way to accept her death at the hands of Ikari. This made the interpretation of "Liar" very important. But the voice-over grew nearer and nearer.... Director Anno must have noticed how I felt. When it came time to do the voice-over, he showed me a single, hidden hint at the last moment. With that one incredible hint, I, and Ritsuko Akagi, were utterly defeated. It hardly needs saying, but Director Anno is incredible. Truly awesome -- a genius. So Gendo does indeed have a line there. Gendo did tell Ritsuko something, it's not simply a matter of Anno trying to be clever or leave the audience wondering. What could Gendo have said? Many of theorized it was simply, "I love/loved you." - but that seems too clean and simple, too trite, for Anno to make such a big deal about protecting the secret. Love is the obvious answer - and Gendo was never obvious. Personally, I'm content in believing Gendo said, "Ritsuko Akagi, I truly... needed you."

[]:             Quote Post by MongolSquad » Sat Aug 21, 2004 4:16 pm I've seen it, I was hoping for some other and new ideas and discussion, not just a simple answer.....

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Aug 21, 2004 11:06 pm Apparently, Shin-Seiki over at the Anime Nation forum has a ground shattering new theory about this. Perhaps he'll come out with it soon and shed some light on this. As for me... Ritsuko was supposedly very much against relationships, she even says that they are illogical. So some believe her love affair with him was simply for sexual gratification. I think it would be fitting that she would actually fall in love with Gendo in the process, hence her intense jealousy and anger towards him towards the end of the series. His line, in my opinion has more to do with that than anything else. I doubt Gendo said he loved her. His love for Yui was supposed to be profound. He manipulates people, he manipulated her though the act of sex. I believe what he said had to do with needing her (as stated by Eva Otaku), or perhaps, similarly: "Ritsuko Akagi, I truly am sorry." Though he manipulated her for his own means, he felt guilt, and wanted to express that, in much the same way that he expressed his guilt over neglecting Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Aug 22, 2004 7:47 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Apparently, Shin-Seiki over at the Anime Nation forum has a ground shattering new theory about this. Perhaps he'll come out with it soon and shed some light on this.
